subject: Product Management
title: The iPhone
description: A quick win: understand The iPhone.

hook.text: In 2007, Steve Jobs introduced 'a widescreen iPod, a phone, and an internet device.' They were all one product.

buildup.text: Existing smartphones had tiny screens, physical keyboards, and styluses. The iPhone had a 3.5-inch touchscreen and no keyboard.

discovery.text: The product breakthrough wasn't any single feature — it was combining music, phone, and web into one seamless experience.

twist.text: Competitors laughed. Steve Ballmer said nobody would buy a $500 phone. BlackBerry engineers dismissed the touchscreen.

climax.text: The iPhone didn't just win the phone market. It created the app economy, killing cameras, GPS devices, and MP3 players.

punchline.text: The best products don't improve a category. They replace it.

quiz.question: What made the iPhone a category-defining product?
options: Combining music, phone, and web into one seamless touchscreen experience
